content.text: Breathing marks are found over initial vowels. A smooth breathing mark has no sound and the word will be pronounced beginning simply with the sound of the vowel or diphthong.

An apparent exception is that an initial iota followed by another vowel is pronounced with a 'y' sound, as in the Greek name of Jesus, Ἰησοῦς.  However, this is because the iota is followed by another vowel and they do not form a diphthong, not because of the breathing mark.

A rough breathing mark is pronounced with an h sound. The one exception to this is that a rough breathing mark can also occur before a rho, but is not pronounced with an h sound before the r sound of the rho.

rows: No H sound; word starts with the vowel sound
Exception: the H sound is not pronounced; the word begins with an R sound
